name: What if Perplexity says fair use? Will I lose like in other AI cases?
description: Courts rejected so far; outputs harm market unlike pure training.[1][4]

mainEntity.name: What if Perplexity says fair use? Will I lose like in other AI cases?

acceptedAnswer.text: Courts rejected so far; outputs harm market unlike pure training.[1][4]
acceptedAnswer.description: Fair use unlikely succeeds here; NY courts denied Perplexity's motions, unlike some training-only cases, due to direct reproduction.[1][4] Publishers argue commercial substitution, not transformative.[2][3] Key factors: Amount copied (extensive), market harm (traffic loss), no criticism. Tribune notes verbatim outputs.[6] Prepare rebuttal evidence. Risk: Costly litigation. Strong claims proceed nationwide.
